terms.definition: Beta-lactam antibiotic class derived from Penicillium moulds; first-line for many streptococcal infections.
terms.exampleSentence: Have you ever had a reaction to penicillin or any other antibiotic?
terms.contextNotes: Always document allergy details, not just the word 'allergy'.

terms.definition: Broad-spectrum aminopenicillin; commonly used for chest, ENT and dental infections.
terms.exampleSentence: Take one amoxicillin 500 mg capsule three times a day for five days, and complete the course.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid in glandular fever — risk of widespread rash.

terms.definition: Amoxicillin combined with clavulanic acid (beta-lactamase inhibitor); broader Gram-negative cover.
terms.exampleSentence: Co-amoxiclav 625 mg three times daily was prescribed for the infected diabetic foot ulcer.
terms.contextNotes: Higher risk of cholestatic jaundice than plain amoxicillin.

terms.definition: Penicillinase-resistant penicillin; first-line for staphylococcal skin and soft tissue infections.
terms.exampleSentence: Take flucloxacillin one hour before food or two hours after, four times daily for cellulitis.
terms.contextNotes: Counsel on absorption — empty stomach essential.

terms.definition: Beta-lactam antibiotic class organised by generations; broad-spectrum cover with renal excretion.
terms.exampleSentence: Ceftriaxone, a third-generation cephalosporin, was started intravenously for suspected meningitis.
terms.contextNotes: Cross-reactivity with penicillin allergy is low but possible.

terms.definition: Antibiotic class (e.g. clarithromycin, erythromycin); useful in penicillin allergy and atypical pneumonia.
terms.exampleSentence: Clarithromycin, a macrolide, was prescribed because she reported a penicillin allergy.
terms.contextNotes: QT prolongation risk; many CYP3A4 interactions.

terms.definition: Broad-spectrum antibiotic class (e.g. ciprofloxacin, levofloxacin); restricted use due to safety alerts.
terms.exampleSentence: Ciprofloxacin, a fluoroquinolone, should be reserved for serious infections per MHRA guidance.
terms.contextNotes: Risk of tendon rupture, aortic aneurysm and disabling neuropsychiatric effects.

terms.definition: Broad-spectrum antibiotic class (e.g. doxycycline); avoid in pregnancy and children under 12.
terms.exampleSentence: Doxycycline, a tetracycline, is taken with a full glass of water and the patient should remain upright for 30 minutes.
terms.contextNotes: Causes photosensitivity; avoid antacids/iron at the same time.

terms.definition: Bactericidal antibiotic class (e.g. gentamicin); requires therapeutic drug monitoring.
terms.exampleSentence: Gentamicin, an aminoglycoside, requires trough level monitoring to minimise nephrotoxicity.
terms.contextNotes: Ototoxic and nephrotoxic; check renal function before each dose.

terms.definition: Antibiotic class often combined with trimethoprim as co-trimoxazole (Septrin).
terms.exampleSentence: Co-trimoxazole, a sulfonamide combination, is used for PJP prophylaxis in immunocompromised patients.
terms.contextNotes: Risk of Stevens-Johnson syndrome and severe skin reactions.

terms.definition: Drug class for fungal infections; topical or systemic (e.g. fluconazole, terbinafine, clotrimazole).
terms.exampleSentence: Topical clotrimazole, an antifungal cream, is applied twice daily for two weeks for athlete's foot.
terms.contextNotes: Many azoles inhibit CYP3A4 — significant interaction risk.

terms.definition: Drug class targeting viral replication (e.g. aciclovir for HSV/VZV; oseltamivir for influenza).
terms.exampleSentence: Aciclovir, an antiviral, should be started within 72 hours of shingles rash onset.
terms.contextNotes: Dose-adjust aciclovir in renal impairment.

terms.definition: Drug class for HIV; combination therapy (cART) blocks viral replication at multiple stages.
terms.exampleSentence: Adherence to antiretroviral therapy is essential to prevent resistance and maintain viral suppression.
terms.contextNotes: Significant interactions with PPIs, antacids, statins and rifampicin.

terms.definition: Nitroimidazole antibiotic active against anaerobes and protozoa; used for C. difficile and BV.
terms.exampleSentence: Avoid alcohol during and for 48 hours after a course of metronidazole due to a disulfiram-like reaction.
terms.contextNotes: Disulfiram-like reaction with alcohol is the key counselling point.

terms.definition: Prescribing abbreviation 'pro re nata' meaning 'as required'; specify max dose and frequency.
terms.exampleSentence: Paracetamol 1 g PRN, maximum four times in 24 hours, was added to the drug chart.
terms.contextNotes: PRN entries must always state indication and maximum frequency.

terms.definition: Prescribing abbreviation 'bis die' meaning twice daily.
terms.exampleSentence: Take one tablet BD, morning and evening, for two weeks.
terms.contextNotes: Spell out frequencies on patient labels — 'twice a day'.

terms.definition: Prescribing abbreviation 'ter die sumendum' meaning three times daily.
terms.exampleSentence: Amoxicillin 500 mg capsules, take one TDS for five days.
terms.contextNotes: Equivalent of TID in US prescribing.

terms.definition: Prescribing abbreviation 'quater die sumendum' meaning four times daily.
terms.exampleSentence: Paracetamol 1 g QDS regularly, with a 24-hour maximum of 4 g.
terms.contextNotes: Spaced roughly every six hours when scheduled.

terms.definition: Latin abbreviation meaning 'in the morning'; used on prescriptions for once-daily morning dosing.
terms.exampleSentence: Levothyroxine 100 micrograms one tablet mane, half an hour before breakfast.
terms.contextNotes: Translate to plain English on the dispensing label.

terms.definition: Latin abbreviation meaning 'at night'; used on prescriptions for evening dosing.
terms.exampleSentence: Atorvastatin 20 mg one tablet nocte for hyperlipidaemia.
terms.contextNotes: Translate to 'at bedtime' on the patient label.

terms.definition: Weight-based dose unit (milligrams per kilogram of body weight); essential for paediatric dosing.
terms.exampleSentence: Paracetamol oral suspension is dosed at 15 mg/kg every four to six hours in children.
terms.contextNotes: Always confirm a current weight, not a historical one.

terms.definition: To adjust dose upward or downward in steps to achieve a target response while minimising side effects.
terms.exampleSentence: We will titrate her ramipril dose every two weeks based on blood pressure and U&Es.
terms.contextNotes: Always specify the steps and the target endpoint.

terms.definition: Gradual dose reduction to avoid withdrawal or rebound (e.g. corticosteroids, opioids, SSRIs).
terms.exampleSentence: After two weeks on prednisolone 30 mg, we will taper by 5 mg every five days.
terms.contextNotes: Document the schedule clearly on discharge summaries.

terms.definition: Initial higher dose to rapidly reach therapeutic concentration before maintenance dosing.
terms.exampleSentence: She received a 300 mg loading dose of clopidogrel followed by 75 mg once daily.
terms.contextNotes: Common with digoxin, amiodarone, vancomycin, clopidogrel.

terms.definition: Ongoing dose required to maintain therapeutic drug concentrations after the loading phase.
terms.exampleSentence: After the loading dose, the maintenance dose of digoxin is 125 micrograms once daily.
terms.contextNotes: Adjust in renal or hepatic impairment as required.

terms.definition: Plasma drug concentration window producing efficacy without unacceptable toxicity.
terms.exampleSentence: Digoxin has a narrow therapeutic range, so we monitor levels and watch for toxicity.
terms.contextNotes: Drugs with narrow range: digoxin, lithium, phenytoin, warfarin.

terms.definition: Minimum inhibitory concentration — lowest antibiotic concentration that prevents visible bacterial growth.
terms.exampleSentence: Vancomycin MIC of 1 mg/L confirmed the isolate was susceptible.
terms.contextNotes: Compared against EUCAST breakpoints in the UK.

terms.definition: Folate-pathway antibiotic; first-line for uncomplicated lower urinary tract infection in many UK areas.
terms.exampleSentence: Trimethoprim 200 mg BD for three days is recommended for an uncomplicated UTI in non-pregnant women.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid in first trimester (folate antagonist).

terms.definition: Urinary antibiotic concentrated in urine; first-line for uncomplicated UTI in many UK areas.
terms.exampleSentence: Take nitrofurantoin with food to reduce nausea, and complete the three-day course.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid if eGFR <45 mL/min/1.73 m²; risk of pulmonary fibrosis with long-term use.

terms.definition: Glycopeptide antibiotic for serious Gram-positive infections including MRSA; trough levels needed.
terms.exampleSentence: IV vancomycin trough should be measured before the third or fourth dose.
terms.contextNotes: 'Red man syndrome' if infused too quickly.
